Teaching

On this page

  • Strategic Management
  • Social Network Analysis in Educational Research
  • Introductory Research Seminar
  • Research Seminar “Quantitative and Qualitative methods”
  • Data Analysis for managers
  • Organizational Theory and Behavior

Below is a selection of courses I have designed and taught.

Strategic Management

Explore strategic management across organizational functions and learn how environmental factors shape business decisions in a global context

KU Leuven | Bachelor | 140 students | TA, grading exams | 2025-2026

Social Network Analysis in Educational Research

Use R, Gephi, ORA, and the basics of social network analysis to tackle research questions and managerial challenges in educational institutions.

HSE University | Master | 20 students | Course curriculum designer and instructor | 2021

Introductory Research Seminar

Develop essential managerial soft skills; showcase and explore career tracks for undergraduate students in international business and management via case championships (Unilever, Upteam, Arrival) and meetings with alumni

HSE University | Bachelor | 180-270 students | Course curriculum designer and instructor | 2019-2022

Research Seminar “Quantitative and Qualitative methods”

Plan and execute rigorous quantitative and qualitative research in academic and industry domains.

HSE University | Bachelor/Master | 20-270 students | Course curriculum designer and instructor | 2016-2021

Data Analysis for managers

Learn R, SPSS, Stata, Python, Tableau to execute statistical analysis and visualisations, and essentially make sense of various data types.

HSE University | Bachelor/Master | 20-130 students | Course curriculum designer and instructor | 2016-2021

Organizational Theory and Behavior

Study the structure and dynamic nature of organisations and their impact on society and individuals

HSE University | Bachelor | 110-200 students | Course curriculum designer and instructor | 2012-2017
